subtitle: Track daily expenses & stay on budget

views.name: DashboardView
views.purpose: Main dashboard showing today's spending total vs daily budget as a progress ring, top spending categories as a horizontal bar breakdown, and a scrollable list of today's recent entries with quick-add FAB

views.name: EntrySheet
views.purpose: Quick expense entry modal with prominent amount stepper ($0.50 increments), category grid picker with icons, payment method selector, recurring toggle, and optional note field

views.name: HistoryView
views.purpose: Date-grouped expense list with per-day totals shown in section headers, filterable by category and payment method, with a calendar heatmap showing spending intensity per day (green=under budget, red=over budget)

views.name: AnalyticsView
views.purpose: Multi-chart analytics: donut chart for category breakdown, bar chart for daily spending over past 30 days with budget line overlay, day-of-week average spending pattern, top categories ranked list, and AI-style insight cards highlighting trends

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayTotal
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all expense amounts logged today

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayBudgetProgress
viewModelComputedProperties.description: todayTotal / dailyBudgetGoal clamped to 0...1 for progress ring

viewModelComputedProperties.name: todayEntries
viewModelComputedProperties.description: All entries from today sorted by most recent first

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyTotal
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all expenses in the current Mon-Sun week

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weeklyAverage
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Average daily spending over the past 7 days

viewModelComputedProperties.name: monthlyTotal
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all expenses in the current calendar month

viewModelComputedProperties.name: underBudgetStreak
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Consecutive days where daily total was at or below the daily budget goal, counting backwards from yesterday (today excluded since it's in progress)

viewModelComputedProperties.name: categoryBreakdown
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Each category with its total spending for the selected time period (default: current month), sorted by amount descending

viewModelComputedProperties.name: topCategory
viewModelComputedProperties.description: The category with the highest total spending this month

viewModelComputedProperties.name: dailyTrendData
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Array of (date, dailyTotal) pairs for the past 30 days, used for the bar chart in analytics

viewModelComputedProperties.name: dayOfWeekAverages
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Average spending per day-of-week (Mon through Sun) calculated from all historical data, reveals habitual patterns

viewModelComputedProperties.name: weekOverWeekChange
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Percentage change: (thisWeekTotal - lastWeekTotal) / lastWeekTotal * 100. Positive means spending increased

viewModelComputedProperties.name: spendingFreeDays
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Number of days in the current month with zero expenses logged

viewModelComputedProperties.name: recurringTotal
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Sum of all expenses marked as recurring this month, helps user see fixed vs discretionary spending

viewModelComputedProperties.name: insights
viewModelComputedProperties.description: Array of 2-4 generated insight strings: e.g., 'Dining out is up 35% vs last week', 'You saved $12 on coffee this week', 'Tuesday is your highest spending day', 'You're on a 5-day under-budget streak!'

dailyGoal.description: Daily spending budget ceiling. The progress ring fills as spending approaches this limit. Green when under 70%, yellow 70-100%, red when exceeded. Amount stepper increments by $0.50 for the entry, but the budget goal is set in whole dollars.

domainLogic.streakDefinition: A day counts toward the under-budget streak if the sum of all expenses logged that day is less than or equal to the daily budget goal. Days with zero entries also count (spending-free day = under budget). The streak breaks when any completed day (not today) exceeds the budget.
domainLogic.trendAnalysis: Primary trend: 7-day rolling average compared to the previous 7-day rolling average, expressed as percentage change. Secondary: month-to-date total vs same point last month. Category-level: each category's this-week total vs last-week total. Day-of-week pattern: flag the user's historically most expensive day.
insights: Week-over-week total change: 'You spent X% more/less this week vs last week'
Category spike detection: if any category's weekly total is >30% above its 4-week average, alert the user
Under-budget streak celebration: 'Amazing! 7-day streak of staying under $50/day'
Spending-free day count: 'You had 3 no-spend days this month — nice discipline!'
Top category awareness: 'Dining out accounts for 34% of your monthly spending'
Day-of-week pattern: 'Saturdays average $67 — your most expensive day'
Recurring vs discretionary: 'Fixed expenses are $X (Y% of monthly total)'
Budget pace: 'At this rate, you'll spend $X by month end (budget: $Y)'
